13:07
Crypto Rug Muncher
The news of a wrench attack on trader Sillytuna is a grim reality check for us all. Being targeted, kidnapped, assaulted, and losing $24M AUD proves that code can't protect you from physical violence, only you can.

If you’re holding significant crypto assets, your front door is your biggest vulnerability.

Here are some ways to keep yourself safe.

Silence is safety: Stop broadcasting wins, your location, or your lifestyle. Privacy is your first line of defense. If they don't know you have it, they won't come for it.

Remove the single point of failure: Use multisig setups (like Gnosis Safe) that require remote signers. If you can move the funds alone, a kidnapper can make you do it.

Off-site custody: Keep hardware wallets and backup seeds in a bank vault or a secure location away from where you sleep.

Decoy Wallets: Use devices (like ColdCard or Ledger) with a duress PIN. One PIN opens your real stash; the "duress" one opens a decoy with a believable but small amount.

Digital Hygiene: Use a burner phone for crypto and never link your real-world identity (KYC) to your main cold storage addresses.

Digital footprints: Audit your data. If your real-world identity is linked to your public wallet or leaked in a KYC hack, you are searchable.

In a world of irreversible transactions, you are the highest-value target. Practice the best op-sec you can so that you don't become a victim.

13:24
Crypto Rug Muncher
While the crypto markets are showing some green right now, it is vital to stay grounded and consider the broader market cycle we are currently in. In a typical bear market year, it is actually quite common to see short-term, aggressive rallies that look like a full recovery. However, these are often "sucker’s rallies", temporary price spikes that lure investors back in before even more significant dumps occur later in the year.

I can’t say with absolute certainty that this is exactly how things will play out this year, but it is a historical pattern that shouldn't be ignored. Amidst all the noise from the crypto bros claiming that "we are so back," remember that a rally during a downtrend isn't always a reversal. It’s often just a breather before the next leg down and a continuation of the trend. Stay cautious, manage your risk, and don't let FOMO dictate your strategy.
C
13:53
Crypto Rug Muncher
My Stance on the GENIUS Act

Some folks are misinterpreting my thoughts on the GENIUS Act, so let me be clear: I am not opposed to people earning a yield on their stablecoins. Not in the slightest. What I am opposed to is a lopsided system where consumers shoulder 100% of the risk for earning that yield while the companies facilitating it assume zero.

In a functional regulatory environment, you want systems that incentivize companies to provide the absolute best, most resilient products possible. You want protections baked into the framework, not added as an afterthought.

The GENIUS Act falls short because it prioritizes corporate shielding over individual security. While it technically prohibits issuers from paying interest directly, it leaves massive loopholes for "affiliate" and "third-party" arrangements, essentially allowing companies to offload risky yield-generating activities onto other entities while keeping their own balance sheets "compliant." If things fall apart, who is going to take responsibility for that risk besides the consumer?

If you are a retail investor, you want policy that advocates for you and protects your assets if things go sideways. As it stands, this bill protects the companies who are designing the next generation of market failures, rather than the people who provide these products with their actual value.
C
14:09
Crypto Rug Muncher
Another day, another insider exploiting national security secrets on prediction markets for financial gain.

We used to call this espionage. We used to hang people for trading off state secrets for their own benefit. Now, we celebrate their win rates on Polymarket and call it "alpha."

When did treason become a side hustle? When did the lives of soldiers and the stability of nations become event contracts?

We’ve traded our national security for the benefit of those who want to profit from it.

Let's go back to the old way. Hold them accountable, strip the profit, and bring back the consequences that actually match the seriousness of the crime.

15:01
Crypto Rug Muncher
The $CRM - $cryptorugmunch Ethos: Stop Being Exit Liquidity

The Game is Rigged (By Design)

If you’re a retail investor, you’ve felt it: that nagging sense that you’re always the last to know. That isn’t an accident. The "Elite"—the VCs, the institutional players, and the media outlets they fund—have turned crypto into a machine designed to extract value from you. They own the microphones, they craft the narratives, and they use industry spin to lead you into trades that benefit them, not you. They want you hyped up and just uneducated enough to buy their bags.

We believe the only way to win a rigged game is to stop playing by its rules. You shouldn’t just be "investing"; you need to be advocating for your own interests. If you aren't educated enough to spot a psyop the moment it hits your feed, you'll find yourself accidentally pushing the narrative of the very people who steal from you every day.

Arming the Common Man

$CRM - $cryptorugmunch is the antidote to the industry’s manufactured hype. We aren’t here to play nice with the insiders or polish their reputations. Our mission is to arm the everyday investor with the raw, unfiltered analysis and the on-chain security tools needed to survive this space.

Words aren't enough, you need utility. That’s why we are building security tools to help you verify the truth for yourself, rather than trusting a paid influencer's "expert" opinion. Our promise is simple: We refuse to push any agenda against the people we are building for, and we refuse to hide the truth behind industry spin. We don’t have corporate masters, so we can call out the scams, the traps, and the bad actors that others are paid to ignore.

Keeping Crypto Free

Supporting $CRM is a vote for a different kind of crypto culture—one where retail isn't just an afterthought but central to the industry. We are fighting for independent media, transparent development, and a market where information isn't a weapon used against you.

Don’t let them control the narrative. Don’t let them drain your bags. Join us in keeping crypto what it was always meant to be: a tool for financial freedom for all, not a playground for the 1%.

Support $CRM. Use the Tools. Know the Truth. Stay Free.
